title: INNER JOIN witnesses in spendable_notes is semantically correct — not an optimization
description: spendable_notes() uses INNER JOIN witnesses rather than LEFT JOIN or fetching witness_data separately. This is not a performance choice — it is a correctness constraint: a note without a witness cannot be spent. Including it via LEFT JOIN would return witness_data = NULL, which witness_to_merkle_path would then reject as WitnessDeserialize('empty witness data'), producing a confusing runtime error deep inside build_shielded_tx. The INNER JOIN makes the invariant 'spendable_notes always has a valid witness' hold at the query boundary rather than relying on callers to re-check. A contributor who changes this to LEFT JOIN 'to be more complete' is introducing a latent crash on any note the scanner has received but not yet witnessed.
close_reason: decision records documented in beads; no code change required
